title: Feather marbling pattern — the peacock and snail combs
sources: Josef Halfer, The Progress of the Marbling Art (1893, public domain) — feather and peacock patterns; C.W. Woolnough, The Art of Marbling (1881 expanded edition, public domain) — combed variants
notes: Starting from a nonpareil base, a single-tooth stylus drawn through the bands at intervals creates the feather pattern. Pulling in one direction gives a simple feather; alternating the direction of each stroke gives the 'snail' or 'peacock tail' pattern. Requires good colour contrast in the base pattern for the feather to read clearly.
